4.20: Our ancestors worshiped on this mountain, but you say that the place where people must worship is in Jerusalem.»
4.21: Jesus said, «Woman, believe me, the hour is coming when neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem will you worship the Father.
4.22: You worship what you do not know; we worship what we know, for salvation is from the Jews.
4.23: But the hour is near and has already come, when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and in truth; it is such worshipers that the Father seeks.
4.24: God is spirit, and those who worship him must worship him in spirit and in truth.»
4.25: The woman replied, "I know that the Messiah, who is called Christ, is coming. When he comes, he will teach us all things."«
4.26: Jesus said to him, "I am he, the one speaking to you."«
4.27: At that moment his disciples arrived and were astonished that he was speaking with a woman; however, none of them said, «What do you want?» or «Why are you speaking with her?»
4.28: The woman then left her jug, went into the town, and said to the inhabitants:
4.29: «"Come and see a man who told me what I did; could it not be Christ?"»
4.30: They left the city and came to him.
4.31: During the interval, his disciples urged him, saying, "Master, eat."«
4.32: But he said to them, "I have food to eat that you know nothing about."«
4.33: And the disciples said to one another, «Could someone have brought him something to eat?»
4.34: Jesus said to them, «My food is to do the will of him who sent me and to accomplish his work.
4.35: Do you not yourselves say, 'Four more months and it will be harvest'? I tell you, open your eyes and see the fields! They are already white for harvest.
4.36: The reaper receives his wages and gathers fruit for eternal life, so that the sower and the reaper may rejoice together.
4.37: For here the adage applies: One is the sower and another the reaper.
4.38: I sent you to reap what you did not work for; others did the work, and you entered into their labor.»
4.39: Now many Samaritans from that town believed in Jesus because of the testimony of the woman who said, «He told me everything I ever did.»
4.40: So the Samaritans came to him and asked him to stay with them, and he stayed there for two days.
4.41: And a greater number believed in him because they heard him himself.
4.42: And they said to the woman, «Now we no longer believe because of what you said, for we have heard for ourselves, and we know that this is truly the Savior of the world.»
4.43: After these two days, Jesus left there and went to Galilee.
4.44: For Jesus himself had declared that a prophet is not honored in his own country.
4.45: When he arrived in Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him, having seen all that he had done in Jerusalem during the festival.,
4.46: for they too had gone to the feast. So he returned to Cana in Galilee, where he had turned the water into wine. Now there was a royal official whose son was sick in Capernaum.
4.47: Having heard that Jesus was coming from Judea to Galilee, he went to him and begged him to come down and heal his son who was dying.
4.48: Jesus said to him, «Unless you see signs and wonders, you will not believe.»
4.49: The king's officer said to him, "Lord, come before my child dies.
4.50: »Go,” Jesus replied, “your child is full of life.” The man believed what Jesus said to him and went away.
4.51: As he was returning, his servants came to meet him and told him that his child was alive.
4.52: He asked them at what time he had felt better, and they told him, "Yesterday, at the seventh hour, the fever left him."«
4.53: The father recognized that this was the hour at which Jesus had said to him, "Your son is full of life," and he and all his household believed.
4.54: This was the second miracle that Jesus performed on his return from Judea to Galilee.

5.1: After this there was a Jewish festival and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
5.2: Now in Jerusalem, near the Sheep Gate, there is a pool which is called in Hebrew Bethesda and which has five porticoes.
5.3: Under these porticoes lay a large number of sick, blind, lame and paralyzed people. [They were waiting for the water to boil.
5.4: For an angel of the Lord went down at certain times into the pool and stirred up the water. And whoever stepped in first after the stirring of the water was healed of whatever infirmity he had.
5.5: There was a man who had been ill for thirty-eight years.
5.6: When Jesus saw him lying there and knew that he had been ill for a long time, he said to him:
5.7: «Do you want to be healed?» The sick man replied, «Lord, I have no one to put me into the pool as soon as the water is stirred, and while I am going, another steps down before me.»
5.8: Jesus said to him, «Get up, take your mat and walk.»
5.9: And instantly the man was healed, he took up his stretcher and began to walk. It was a Sabbath day.
5.10: So the Jews said to the man who had been healed, «It is the Sabbath; you are not permitted to carry your stretcher.»
5.11: He replied, "The one who healed me told me: Take your stretcher and walk."«
5.12: They asked him, "Who is the man who told you: 'Take your stretcher and walk'?"«
5.13: But the one who had been healed did not know who it was, because Jesus had slipped away, thanks to the crowd that was in that place.
5.14: Later, Jesus found him in the temple and said to him, "See, you are well again. Sin no more, lest something worse happen to you."«
5.15: This man went away and told the Jews that it was Jesus who had healed him.
5.16: That is why the Jews persecuted Jesus because he did these things on the Sabbath day.
5.17: But Jesus said to them, «My Father is always at work to this very day, and I too am working.»
5.18: Because of this, the Jews sought all the more earnestly to kill him, because, not only was he breaking the Sabbath, but he was also saying that God was his Father, making himself equal with God. Jesus then answered them:
5.19: «Truly, truly, I say to you, the Son can do nothing of himself, but only what he sees the Father doing; and whatever the Father does, the Son also does likewise.
5.20: For the Father loves the Son and shows him all that he does, and he will show him works greater than these, which will astonish you.
5.21: For as the Father raises the dead and gives life, so the Son gives life to whom he wills.
5.22: For the Father himself judges no one, but has given the Son all judgment,
5.23: so that all may honor the Son as they honor the Father. Whoever does not honor the Son does not honor the Father who sent him.
5.24: Truly, truly, I say to you, whoever hears my word and believes him who sent me has eternal life and does not incur condemnation, but has passed from death to life.
5.25: Truly, truly, I say to you, the hour is coming and is now here when the dead will hear the voice of the Son of God, and those who hear it will live.
5.26: For as the Father has life in himself, so he has granted the Son to have life in himself,
5.27: And he also gave him the authority to judge, because he is the Son of Man.
5.28: Do not be amazed at this, for the hour is coming when all those in the tombs will hear his voice.
5.29: And those who have done good will come out to a resurrection of life, and those who have done evil to a resurrection of condemnation.
5.30: I can do nothing on my own. As I hear, I judge, and my judgment is just, because I do not seek my own will, but the will of him who sent me.
5.31: If I am the one giving testimony about myself, my testimony is not truthful.
5.32: There is another who testifies about me, and I know that the testimony he gives about me is true.
5.33: You sent to John, and he bore witness to the truth.
5.34: For me, it is not from a man that I receive testimony, but I say this so that you may be saved.
5.35: Jean was the lamp that burns and shines, but you only wanted to rejoice for a moment in its light.
5.36: For I have a greater testimony than that of John, for the works which the Father has given me to accomplish, these very works which I do, testify about me that it is the Father who sent me.
5.37: And the Father who sent me has himself testified about me. You have never heard his voice, nor seen his face.
5.38: And you do not have his word remaining in you, because you do not believe in the one he sent.
5.39: You scrutinize the Scriptures because you think you will find eternal life in them,
5.40: Yet it is they who bear witness to me, and you do not want to come to me to have life.
5.41: It is not that I seek my glory from men.
5.42: But I know you, I know that you do not have the love of God within you.
5.43: I have come in my Father's name and you do not receive me; let another come in his own name, and you will receive him.
5.44: How can you believe, you who receive glory from one another and do not seek the glory that comes from God alone?
5.45: Do not think that it is I who will accuse you before the Father; your accuser is Moses, in whom you have placed your hope.
5.46: For if you believed Moses, you would believe me also, because he wrote about me.
5.47: But if you don't believe his writings, how will you believe my words?»

6.1: Jesus then went to the other side of the Sea of Galilee or of Tiberias.
6.2: And a large crowd followed him, because they saw the miracles he performed on those who were sick.
6.3: Jesus went up on the mountain and there he sat with his disciples.
6.4: But Passover, the Jewish festival, was approaching.
6.5: Jesus, therefore, when he looked up and saw a large crowd coming towards him, said to Philip, «Where shall we buy bread for these people to eat?»
6.6: He said this to test her, because he knew what he had to do.
6.7: Philippe replied, "Two hundred denarii worth of bread would not be enough for everyone to receive a piece."«
6.8: One of his disciples, Andrew, brother of Simon Peter, said to him:
6.9: «"There is a young man here who has five barley loaves and two fish, but what is that to so many people?"»
6.10: Jesus said, «Have them sit down.» Now there was plenty of grass in that place. So they sat down, about five thousand in number.
6.11: Jesus took the loaves and, having given thanks, he distributed them to those who were seated, and likewise gave them as much of the two fish as they wanted.
6.12: When they had had enough to eat, he said to his disciples, «Gather the pieces that are left over, so that nothing is wasted.»
6.13: They gathered them up and filled twelve baskets with the pieces that remained of the five barley loaves after they had eaten.
6.14: When these men saw the miracle that Jesus had performed, they said, "This is truly the Prophet who is to come into the world."«
6.15: Knowing that they were going to come and take him away to make him king, Jesus withdrew again, alone, to the mountain.
6.16: When evening came, the disciples went down to the seashore,
6.17: And having gotten into a boat, they crossed the sea in the direction of Capernaum. It was already night and Jesus had not yet joined them.
6.18: However, the sea, whipped up by a strong wind, was rough.
6.19: When they had rowed about twenty-five to thirty stadia, they saw Jesus walking on the sea and approaching the boat, and they were afraid.
6.20: But he said to them, "It is I; do not be afraid."«
6.21: So they wanted to take him into the boat, and immediately the boat was at the place where they were going.
6.22: The following day, the crowd that had remained on the other side of the sea noticed that there was only one boat there and that Jesus had not entered it with his disciples, but that they had left alone.
6.23: Other boats, however, had arrived from Tiberias near the place where the Lord, after giving thanks, had given them food.
6.24: So when the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and went to Capernaum to look for Jesus.
6.25: And when they found him on the other side of the sea, they said to him, «Master, when did you come here?»
6.26: Jesus answered them, «Truly, truly, I say to you, you are looking for me, not because you saw miracles, but because you ate loaves and were satisfied.
6.27: Do not work for the food that perishes, but for the food that endures to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give you. For on him God the Father has set his seal.»
6.28: They said to him, «What must we do to perform the works of God?»
6.29: Jesus answered them, «This is the work God requires: that you believe in the one he has sent.»
6.30: They said to him, «What sign then will you perform that we may see it and believe in you? What are your works?”
6.31: Our ancestors ate the manna in the desert, as it is written: »He gave them bread from heaven to eat.”
6.32: Jesus answered them, «Truly, truly, I say to you, it was not Moses who gave you the bread from heaven, but it is my Father who gives the true bread from heaven.
6.33: For the bread of God is the bread that comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.»
6.34: So they said to him, «Lord, give us this bread always.»
6.35: Jesus answered them, «I am the bread of life: whoever comes to me will never go hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.
6.36: But, as I told you, you saw me and you don't believe it.
6.37: All that the Father gives me will come to me, and whoever comes to me I will never drive away.
6.38: For I have come down from heaven, not to do my own will, but the will of him who sent me.
6.39: But it is the will of him who sent me that I should lose none of those he has given me, but raise them up on the last day.
6.40: For it is the will of my Father [who sent me], that everyone who sees the Son and believes in him should have eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day.»
6.41: The Jews grumbled about him because he had said, "I am the living bread that came down from heaven."«
6.42: And they said, «Is this not Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How then can he say, »I have come down from heaven’?”
6.43: Jesus answered them, «Do not murmur among yourselves.
6.44: No one can come to me unless the Father who sent me draws him, and I will raise him up at the last day.
6.45: It is written in the Prophets: They will all be taught by God. Everyone who has heard the Father and received his teaching comes to me.
6.46: Not that anyone has seen the Father, except the one who is of God; he has seen the Father.
6.47: Truly, truly, I say to you, whoever believes in me has eternal life.
6.48: I am the bread of life.
6.49: Your ancestors ate the manna in the desert and they died.
6.50: This is the bread that came down from heaven, so that we may eat of it and not die.
6.51: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. If anyone eats of this bread, he will live forever, and the bread that I will give for the salvation of the world is my flesh.»
6.52: Then the Jews disputed among themselves, saying, «How can this man give his flesh to eat?»
6.53: Jesus said to them, «Truly, truly, I say to you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
6.54: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day.
6.55: For my flesh is truly food and my blood is truly drink.
6.56: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me, and I in him.
6.57: As the living Father sent me and I live because of the Father, so the one who eats me will also live because of me.
6.58: This is the bread that came down from heaven. Unlike your ancestors who ate the manna and died, whoever eats this bread will live forever.»
6.59: Jesus said these things while teaching in the synagogue at Capernaum.
6.60: Many of his disciples, when they heard it, said, "This is a hard saying; who can accept it?"«
6.61: Jesus, knowing in himself that his disciples were murmuring about this, said to them, «Does this offend you?
6.62: And when you see the Son of Man ascending to where he was before?
6.63: It is the Spirit who gives life; the flesh profits nothing. The words that I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
6.64: But there are some among you who do not believe.» For Jesus knew from the beginning who those were who did not believe and who it was who would betray him.
6.65: And he added, «That is why I told you that no one can come to me unless it has been granted them by my Father.»
6.66: From that time on, many of his disciples turned back and no longer followed him.
6.67: Jesus then said to the Twelve, «Do you also want to leave?»
6.68: Simon Peter answered him, «Lord, to whom shall we go? You have the words of eternal life.
6.69: And we have believed and have come to know that you are the Holy One of God.»
6.70: Jesus answered them, «Did I not choose you, the Twelve? And one of you is a devil.»
6.71: He was speaking of Judas, son of Simon Iscariot, for it was he who was to betray him, one of the Twelve.

7.1: After that, Jesus traveled through Galilee, not wanting to go to Judea because the Jews were seeking to kill him.
7.2: Now, the Jewish festival of Tabernacles was approaching.
7.3: So his brothers said to him, «Leave here and go to Judea, so that your disciples also may see the works that you are doing,
7.4: For no one does anything in secret if they want it to be seen. If you do these things, show yourselves to the world.»
7.5: because even his brothers did not believe in him.
7.6: Jesus said to them, «My time has not yet come, but your time is always ready.
7.7: The world cannot hate you, but it hates me, because I bear witness about it that its works are evil.
7.8: "You go up to that party, but I'm not going, because my time hasn't come yet."»
7.9: After saying this, he remained in Galilee.
7.10: But when his brothers had left, he himself also went up to the feast, not publicly, but secretly.
7.11: So the Jews were looking for him during the festival and saying, "Where is he?"«
7.12: And there was a great deal of talk about him among the crowd. Some were saying, "He's a good man." Others were saying, "No, he's deceiving the people."«
7.13: However, no one spoke freely about him, for fear of the Jews.
7.14: We were already in the middle of the festival when Jesus went up to the temple and began to teach.
7.15: The astonished Jews said, "How does he know the Scriptures, since he has not attended school?"«
7.16: Jesus answered them, «My teaching is not my own, but comes from him who sent me.
7.17: If anyone wants to do God's will, he will know whether my teaching is from God, or whether I am speaking of myself.
7.18: He who speaks of himself seeks his own glory, but he who seeks the glory of him who sent him is true, and there is no deceit in him.
7.19: Did not Moses give you the Law? Yet none of you keeps the Law.
7.20: "Why are you trying to kill me?" The crowd replied, "You are possessed by a demon; who is trying to kill you?"»
7.21: Jesus said to them, «I have done one work, and are you all beside yourselves?”
7.22: Moses gave you circumcision, not that it originated with Moses, but with the patriarchs.,
7.23: and you practice it on the Sabbath day. If, in order not to violate the law of Moses, one circumcises on the Sabbath day, how is it that you are indignant with me, because on the Sabbath day I healed a man's whole body?
7.24: Do not judge by appearances, but judge according to justice.»
7.25: Then some of the inhabitants of Jerusalem said, "Isn't this the one they are trying to kill?
7.26: And there he is, speaking publicly without anyone saying a word to him. Would the leaders of the people really have recognized that he is the Christ?
7.27: »This man, however, we know where he comes from, but when Christ comes, no one will know where he comes from.”
7.28: Jesus, teaching in the temple, therefore said aloud: «You know me and you know where I come from, yet I have not come on my own, but he who sent me is true, whom you do not know.
7.29: I know him because I am his son and he is the one who sent me.»
7.30: So they tried to seize him, but no one laid a hand on him, because his time had not yet come.
7.31: But many among the people believed in him, and they said, «When the Christ comes, will he perform more miracles than this man has done?»
7.32: The Pharisees heard the crowd murmuring these things about Jesus, so the chief priests and the Pharisees sent guards to arrest him.
7.33: Jesus said, «I am with you a little while longer, and then I am going to the one who sent me.”.
7.34: You may seek me but you will not find me, and where I am you cannot come.»
7.35: Then the Jews said to one another, «Where will he go that we will not find him? Will he go to the scattered nations and teach them?”
7.36: What does this saying mean, 'You will seek me but you will not find me, and where I am you cannot come'?»
7.37: On the last day of the festival, which is its most solemn day, Jesus stood and said in a loud voice, «If anyone is thirsty, let him come to me and drink.
7.38: "Whoever believes in me, as Scripture has said, rivers of living water will flow from within them."»
7.39: He was saying this about the Spirit, whom those who believed in him were to receive, because the Spirit had not yet been given, since Jesus had not yet been glorified.
7.40: Among the crowd, some who had heard these words said, "This is truly the prophet."«
7.41: Others said: "It is the Christ. But," others said, "is it from Galilee that the Christ must come?"
7.42: Does not Scripture say that the Christ will come from the line of David and from Bethlehem, where David lived?»
7.43: This is how the people were divided about him.
7.44: Some wanted to arrest him, but no one got their hands on him.
7.45: So when the guards returned to the Pontiffs and Pharisees, they said to them, "Why didn't you bring him in?"«
7.46: The guards replied, "No man ever spoke like this man."«
7.47: The Pharisees replied to them, «Have you also been deceived?
7.48: Is there anyone among the leaders of the people who has believed in him? Is there anyone among the Pharisees?
7.49: But this mob, which knows nothing of the Law, they are cursed.»
7.50: Nicodemus, one of them, the one who had come to Jesus by night, said to them:
7.51: «"Does our law condemn a man without first hearing him and without knowing what he has done?"»
7.52: They answered him, «Are you also a Galilean? Examine the Scriptures carefully, and you will see that no prophet arises from Galilee.»
7.53: And they each returned to their own home.

8.1: Jesus went up to the Mount of Olives.
8.2: But at daybreak he returned to the temple and all the people came to him. And he sat down and taught them.
8.3: Then the Scribes and Pharisees brought to him a woman caught in adultery, and having brought him forward, they said to Jesus:
8.4: «"Counsel, this woman was caught in the act of adultery."»
8.5: But Moses, in the Law, commanded us to stone such people. So what do you say?
8.6: They questioned him in this way to test him, so that they might have some charge to bring against him. But Jesus bent down and wrote on the ground with his finger.
8.7: As they continued to question him, he stood up and said to them, "Let he among you who is without sin cast the first stone."«
8.8: And bending down again, he wrote on the ground.
8.9: Having heard this, [and feeling themselves compelled by their conscience] they withdrew one after another, the older ones first, [then all the others] so that Jesus was left alone with the woman who was in the middle.
8.10: Then Jesus stood up and, seeing no one but the woman, said to her, «Woman, where are those who accused you? Has no one condemned you?»
8.11: She replied, "No one, Lord." Jesus said to her, "Neither do I condemn you. Go and sin no more."«
8.12: Jesus spoke to them again, saying, «I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will not walk in darkness, but will have the light of life.»
8.13: To this the Pharisees replied, «You are testifying about yourself, but your testimony is not credible.»
8.14: Jesus answered them, «Although I testify about myself, my testimony is true, because I know where I came from and where I am going, but you do not know where I come from or where I am going.
8.15: You judge according to the flesh, but I judge no one.
8.16: And if I judge, my judgment is true, for I am not alone, but I am with the Father who sent me.
8.17: It is written in your Law that the testimony of two men is trustworthy.
8.18: »I bear witness about myself, and so does the Father who sent me.”
8.19: So they asked him, «Where is your Father?» Jesus answered, «You do not know me or my Father. If you knew me, you would know my Father also.»
8.20: Jesus spoke these words in the courtyard of the treasury, while he was teaching in the temple, and no one laid a hand on him, because his hour had not yet come.
8.21: Jesus said to them again, «I am going away, and you will look for me, and you will die in your sin. Where I am going, you cannot come.»
8.22: The Jews then said, "Is he going to kill himself, since he says, 'Where I am going, you cannot come?'"«
8.23: And he said to them, «You are from below, I am from above; you are of this world, I am not of this world.
8.24: That is why I told you that you will die in your sins, for if you do not believe that I am the Messiah, you will die in your sins.»
8.25: «Who are you?» they asked him. Jesus answered them, «Just what I tell you.”.
8.26: I have much to say about you and much to condemn in you, but the one who sent me is truthful, and what I have heard from him I tell the world.»
8.27: They did not understand that he was talking to them about the Father.
8.28: Jesus then said to them, «When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will know who I am, and that I do nothing on my own authority, but speak just what my Father taught me.
8.29: And the one who sent me is with me and has not left me alone, because I always do what pleases him.»
8.30: Because he said these things, many believed in him.
8.31: So Jesus said to the Jews who had believed in him, «If you abide in my word, you are truly my disciples,
8.32: You will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.»
8.33: They answered him, «We are the descendants of Abraham and have never been slaves of anyone. How can you say, ‘You will become free?’”
8.34: Jesus answered them, «Truly, truly, I say to you, everyone who gives himself over to sin is a slave to sin.
8.35: Now, the slave does not always remain in the house, but the son remains there forever.
8.36: So if the Son sets you free, you will be free indeed.
8.37: I know that you are children of Abraham, but you seek to kill me, because my word does not penetrate you.
8.38: »I tell you what I have seen in my Father’s house, and you do what you have seen in your Father’s house.”
8.39: They answered him, «Our father is Abraham.» Jesus said to them, «If you were Abraham’s children, you would do Abraham’s works.
8.40: But now you seek to kill me, a man who has told you the truth that I heard from God. This is not what Abraham did. You are doing the works of your father.»
8.41: They told him, "We were not born of prostitution; we have one Father, who is God."«
8.42: Jesus said to them, «If God were your Father, you would love me, for I came from God and I am here; I did not come on my own, but he sent me.
8.43: Why don't you recognize my language? Because you cannot hear my speech.
8.44: The father from whom you descend is the devil, and you want to carry out your father’s desires. He was a murderer from the beginning and does not stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he lies, he speaks his native language, for he is a liar and the father of lies.
8.45: And you don't believe me because I'm telling you the truth.
8.46: Which of you will convict me of sin? If I tell the truth, why don't you believe me?
8.47: "Those who belong to God hear God's word; it is because you do not belong to God that you do not hear it."»
8.48: The Jews replied, "Are we not right in saying that you are a Samaritan and possessed by a demon?"«
8.49: Jesus answered, «There is no demon in me, but I honor my Father, and you insult me.
8.50: As for me, I have no concern for my glory: there is someone who takes care of it and who will do justice.
8.51: »Truly, truly, I tell you, if anyone keeps my word, he will never see death.”
8.52: The Jews said to him, «Now we see that a demon is in you. Abraham died, and so did the prophets, yet you say, ‘If anyone keeps my word, he will never see death.’”.
8.53: Are you greater than our father Abraham, who died? The prophets also died; who do you claim to be?»
8.54: Jesus answered, «If I glorify myself, my glory is nothing; it is my Father who glorifies me, the one you call your God.”,
8.55: And yet you do not know him, but I do know him, and if I said I did not know him, I would be a liar like you. But I know him and I keep his word.
8.56: Abraham, your father, rejoiced to see my day; he saw it and was glad.»
8.57: The Jews said to him, "You are not yet fifty years old and you have seen Abraham."«
8.58: Jesus answered them, «Truly, truly, I say to you, before Abraham was, I am.»
8.59: So they picked up stones to throw at him, but Jesus hid himself and left the temple.

9.1: As he passed by, Jesus saw a man blind from birth.
9.2: «Teacher,» his disciples asked, “did this man, or his parents, sin that he was born blind?”
9.3: Jesus answered, «Neither he nor his parents sinned, but this happened so that the works of God might be displayed in him.”.
9.4: While it is day, I must do the works of him who sent me; night is coming, when no one can work.
9.5: While I am in the world, I am the light of the world.»
9.6: Having spoken thus, he spat on the ground, made mud with his saliva, then spread it on the blind man's eyes and said to him:
9.7: «"Go, wash in the pool of Siloam, which means 'Sent'." He went, washed, and returned home seeing clearly.
9.8: The neighbors and those who had seen him before begging were saying, "Isn't this the same man who used to sit and beg?"«
9.9: Some replied, "It's him," others, "No, but he looks like him." But he said, "It's me."«
9.10: So they said to him, «How were your eyes opened?»
9.11: He replied, «A man, called Jesus, made mud and put it on my eyes. He told me, ‘Go to the Pool of Siloam and wash.’ So I went and washed, and I received my sight.”.
9.12: "Where is that man?" they asked him. He replied, "I don't know."»
9.13: They brought to the Pharisees the man who had been blind.
9.14: Now, it was on a Sabbath day that Jesus had made mud and opened the eyes of the blind man.
9.15: In turn, the Pharisees asked him how he had regained his sight, and he told them, "He put mud on my eyes, I washed, and now I see."«
9.16: At this, some of the Pharisees said, «This man is not sent from God, since he does not observe the Sabbath.» Others said, «How can a sinner perform such miracles?» And there was a division among them.
9.17: So they said to the blind man again, "And what do you say about him, that he opened your eyes?" He replied, "He is a prophet."«
9.18: The Jews therefore refused to believe that this man had been blind and had regained his sight, until they had brought in the relatives of the man who had regained his sight.
9.19: They asked them, "Is this your son, whom you say was born blind? How then can he see now?"«
9.20: His parents replied: "We know that this is indeed our son and that he was born blind,
9.21: But how he sees now, we don't know, and who opened his eyes, we don't know. Ask him himself; he's old enough, he'll speak for himself about what concerns him.»
9.22: His parents spoke this way because they feared the Jews. For the Jews had already agreed that anyone who acknowledged Jesus as the Christ would be excluded from the synagogue.
9.23: That is why his parents said, "He is old enough, ask him."«
9.24: The Pharisees brought in the man who had been blind a second time and said to him, «Give glory to God. We know that this man is a sinner.»
9.25: He replied, "Whether he is a sinner, I do not know; I only know that I was blind and now I see."«
9.26: They said to him, "What did he do to you? How did he open your eyes?"«
9.27: He answered them, «I have already told you, and you did not listen: why do you want to hear it again? Do you also want to become his disciples?»
9.28: They then heaped insults upon him and said, "You are his disciple, but we are disciples of Moses.
9.29: We know that God spoke to Moses, but as for this man, we do not know where he came from.»
9.30: The man replied to them: "It is astonishing that you do not know where he is from, and yet he has opened my eyes.
9.31: We know that God does not listen to sinners, but if anyone honors him and does his will, he listens to him.
9.32: We have never heard of anyone opening the eyes of a person born blind.
9.33: If this man were not of God, he could do nothing.»
9.34: They replied, "You were born entirely in sin and you dare to lecture us?" And they drove him out.
9.35: Jesus learned that they had driven him out in this way, and when he found him, he said to him:
9.36: «Do you believe in the Son of Man?» He replied, «Who is he, Lord, that I may believe in him?»
9.37: Jesus said to him, «You have seen him, and the one speaking to you is himself.»
9.38: «"I believe, Lord," he said, and he prostrated himself before him.
9.39: Then Jesus said, «I came into this world for judgment, so that those who do not see may see, and that those who see may become blind.»
9.40: Some Pharisees who were with him said to him, «Are we also blind?»
9.41: Jesus answered them, «If you were blind, you would have no sin; but now you say, »We see,’ your sin remains.”

10.1: «Truly, truly, I say to you, he who does not enter the sheepfold by the door, but climbs in by some other way, is a thief and a robber.
10.2: But the one who enters by the gate is the shepherd of the sheep.
10.3: The gatekeeper opens the gate to him, and the sheep hear his voice; he calls his sheep by name and leads them to pasture.
10.4: When he has brought out all his sheep, he goes ahead of them and the sheep follow him, because they know his voice.
10.5: They will not follow a stranger, but will flee from him, because they do not know the voice of strangers.»
10.6: Jesus told them this allegory, but they did not understand what he was talking about.
10.7: Jesus then said to them again, «Truly, truly, I say to you, I am the gate for the sheep.
10.8: All who came before me were thieves and robbers, but the sheep did not listen to them.
10.9: I am the gate: if anyone enters through me, he will be saved; he will come in and go out, and find pasture.
10.10: The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy; I have come that they may have life, and have it to the full.
10.11: I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for his sheep.
10.12: But the mercenary, who is not the shepherd and to whom the sheep do not belong, sees the wolf coming, abandons the sheep and flees, and the wolf snatches them up and scatters them.
10.13: The mercenary runs away because he is a mercenary and has no concern for the sheep.
10.14: I am the good shepherd, I know my sheep and my sheep know me.,
10.15: As my Father knows me and I know my Father, and I lay down my life for my sheep.
10.16: I have other sheep that are not of this sheepfold; I must bring them also, and they will hear my voice, and there shall be one sheepfold and one shepherd.
10.17: That is why my Father loves me, because I lay down my life to take it up again.
10.18: No one takes it from me, but I lay it down of my own accord. I have authority to lay it down and authority to take it up again. This is the command I received from my Father.»
10.19: This speech again caused division among the Jews.
10.20: Several of them were saying, "He's possessed by a demon, he's delirious: Why are you listening to him?"«
10.21: Others said, "These are not the words of a possessed person; can a demon open the eyes of the blind?"«
10.22: The Feast of Dedication was being celebrated in Jerusalem; it was winter.,
10.23: and Jesus walked around in the temple, under Solomon's Portico.
10.24: The Jews then surrounded him and said, «How long will you keep us in suspense? If you are the Christ, tell us plainly.’
10.25: Jesus answered them, «I told you, and you do not believe me; the works that I do in my Father’s name testify about me,
10.26: But you don't believe me, because you are not one of my sheep.
10.27: My sheep hear my voice. I know them and they follow me.
10.28: And I give them eternal life, and they will never perish, and no one will snatch them out of my hand.
10.29: My Father, who gave them to me, is greater than all, and no one can snatch them out of my Father's hand.
10.30: My father and I are one.»
10.31: The Jews again picked up stones to stone him.
10.32: Jesus said to them, «I have done many good works in your sight, which came from my Father; for which of these works are you going to stone me?»
10.33: The Jews answered him, «We are not stoning you for any good work, but for blasphemy, and because, being a man, you claim to be God,
10.34: Jesus answered them, «Is it not written in your Law, ‘I said, you are gods?’”
10.35: If the Law calls those to whom the word of God came "gods," and if Scripture cannot be broken,
10.36: How can you say to the one whom the Father sanctified and sent into the world, "You are blaspheming because I said, 'I am the Son of God'?"
10.37: If I do not do the works of my Father, do not believe me.
10.38: But if I do them, even if you do not believe me, believe the works themselves, so that you may know and understand that the Father is in me and I am in the Father.»
10.39: Thereupon they again tried to seize him, but he escaped from their grasp.
10.40: He returned across the Jordan to the place where John had begun to baptize, and he stayed there.
10.41: Many came to him, saying, "John performed no miracles, but everything he said about this man was true."«
10.42: And there were many there who believed in him.

11.1: There was a sick man, Lazarus, from Bethany, the village of Mary and Martha, her sister.
11.2: Mary is the one who anointed the Lord with perfume and wiped his feet with her hair, and it was her brother Lazarus who was sick.
11.3: The sisters sent word to Jesus: «Lord, the one you love is sick.»
11.4: Having heard this, Jesus said, «This sickness will not lead to death, but it is for God’s glory so that the Son of God may be glorified through it.»
11.5: Now Jesus loved Martha and her sister Mary and Lazarus.
11.6: Having learned that he was ill, he stayed two more days in the place where he was.
11.7: He then said to his disciples, "Let us return to Judea."«
11.8: The disciples said to him, "Teacher, the Jews were just now trying to stone you, and you're going back there?"«
11.9: Jesus answered, «Are there not twelve hours in a day? If anyone walks during the day, he does not stumble, because he sees the light of this world.
11.10: But if he walks at night, he stumbles because he lacks light.»
11.11: He spoke thus and added: "Our friend Lazarus is asleep, but I am going to wake him up."«
11.12: His disciples told him, "If he sleeps, he will get better."«
11.13: But Jesus had spoken of his death and they thought it was rest from sleep.
11.14: Then Jesus told them plainly, «Lazarus is dead.”
11.15: And I am glad for your sake that I was not there, so that you may believe; but let us go to him.»
11.16: And Thomas, called Didymus, said to the other disciples, «Let us also go, that we may die with him.»
11.17: So Jesus came and found Lazarus had already been in the tomb for four days.
11.18: Now Bethany was near Jerusalem, about fifteen stadia away.
11.19: Many Jews had come to Martha and Mary to comfort them about their brother.
11.20: As soon as Martha heard that Jesus was coming, she went to meet him, while Mary sat at home.
11.21: Martha then said to Jesus, «Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.
11.22: But even now, I know that whatever you ask of God, God will grant it to you.»
11.23: Jesus said to him, «Your brother will rise again.»
11.24: «I know,» Martha replied, “that he will rise again in the resurrection on the last day.”
11.25: Jesus said to him, «I am the resurrection and the life. Whoever believes in me, though he die, yet shall he live,
11.26: And whoever lives and believes in me will never die. Do you believe that?»
11.27: «Yes, Lord,» she told him, «I believe that you are the Christ, the Son of God, who was to come into this world.»
11.28: When she had said this, she went away and secretly called Mary, her sister, saying, "The Teacher is here and is calling for you."«
11.29: As soon as she heard him, she quickly got up and went towards him.
11.30: For Jesus had not yet entered the village; he had not left the place where Martha had met him.
11.31: The Jews who were with Mary, consoling her, saw her get up quickly and leave, and followed her, thinking, "She is going to the tomb to weep there."«
11.32: When Mary arrived at the place where Jesus was, she saw him and fell at his feet, saying, «Lord, if you had been here, my brother would not have died.»
11.33: When Jesus saw her weeping, along with the Jews who were with her, he was deeply moved in spirit and gave in to his emotion.
11.34: And he said, «Where have you put it?» “Lord,” they replied, “come and see.”
11.36: The Jews said, "See how he loved her."«
11.37: But some of them said, "Could not he, who opened the eyes of a man born blind, have also prevented this man from dying?"«
11.38: Jesus, therefore, being deeply moved again, went to the tomb: it was a burial chamber with a stone laid over it.
11.39: «Take away the stone,» Jesus said. Martha, the sister of the dead man, said to him, «Lord, by this time there is a stench, for he has been dead four days.»
11.40: Jesus said to him, «Did I not tell you that if you believe, you will see the glory of God?»
11.41: So they took away the stone and Jesus looked up and said, «Father, I thank you that you have heard me.
11.42: »For my part, I knew that you always answer my prayers, but I said this because of the crowd around me, so that they would believe that you had sent me.”
11.43: Having spoken thus, he shouted in a loud voice:
11.44: «Lazarus, come out.» And the dead man came out, his hands and feet bound with strips of linen, and his face wrapped in a cloth. Jesus said to them, «Take off the grave clothes and let him go.»
11.45: Many of the Jews who had come to Mary and Martha and had seen what Jesus did believed in him.
11.46: But some of them went to the Pharisees and told them what Jesus had done.
11.47: The pontiffs and the Pharisees then assembled the Sanhedrin and said, «What shall we do? For this man performs many miracles.
11.48: If we let him continue, everyone will believe in him, and the Romans will come and destroy our city and our nation.»
11.49: One of them, Caiaphas, who was high priest that year, said to them:
11.50: «"You understand nothing about it; you don't realize that it is in your interest that one man die for the people and that the whole nation not perish."»
11.51: He did not say this of his own accord, but as high priest that year, he prophesied that Jesus would die for the nation
11.52: and not only for the nation, but also to gather into one body the children of God who are scattered abroad.
11.53: From that day on, they deliberated on how to kill him.
11.54: That is why Jesus no longer appeared in public among the Jews, but withdrew to the region near the desert, to a town called Ephrem, and there he stayed with his disciples.
11.55: However, the Jewish Passover was near, and many went up from that region to Jerusalem before Passover to purify themselves.
11.56: They were looking for Jesus and were saying to one another, standing in the temple, «What do you think? Do you think he will not come to the festival?» Now the pontiffs and the Pharisees had given orders that if anyone knew where he was, he should report it, so that they might arrest him.

12.1: Six days before Passover, Jesus came to Bethany, where Lazarus, the dead man whom he had raised from the dead, was.
12.2: There, a dinner was prepared for him, and Martha served. Now Lazarus was among those who were at the table with him.
12.3: Mary, having taken a pound of pure, very precious nard perfume, anointed the feet of Jesus and wiped them with her hair, and the house was filled with the fragrance of the perfume.
12.4: Then one of his disciples, Judas Iscariot, the one who was going to betray him, said:
12.5: «"Why wasn't this perfume sold for three hundred denarii and given to the poor?"»
12.6: He said this, not because he cared about the poor, but because he was a thief and, having the purse, he stole what was put in it.
12.7: Jesus said to her, «Leave her alone; she has kept this perfume for the day of my burial.
12.8: For you will always have the poor with you, but you will not always have me.»
12.9: A large number of Jews learned that Jesus was in Bethany and they came, not only because of Jesus, but also to see Lazarus whom he had raised from the dead.
12.10: But the chief priests decided to put Lazarus to death as well.,
12.11: because many Jews were turning away because of him and believing in Jesus.
12.12: The next day, a multitude of people who had come for the festival, having heard that Jesus was going to Jerusalem, took palm branches and went out to meet him, shouting:
12.13: «"Hosanna. Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord, the King of Israel."»
12.14: Jesus, having found a donkey, mounted it, according to what is written:
12.15: «Do not be afraid, daughter of Zion, for behold, your King is coming, seated on a donkey’s colt.»
12.16: His disciples did not understand these things at first, but when Jesus was glorified, they remembered that these things had been written about him and that he had fulfilled them in his own eyes.
12.17: The crowd that was with him when he called Lazarus from the tomb and raised him from the dead bore witness to him,
12.18: And it was also because she had learned that he had performed this miracle that the multitude had come to meet him.
12.19: The Pharisees then said to one another, "You see that you are gaining nothing: look, everyone is running after him."«
12.20: Now, there were some Greeks among those who had gone up to worship at the festival.
12.21: They approached Philip, who was from Bethsaida in Galilee, and asked him, «Lord, we would like to see Jesus.»
12.22: Philip went to tell Andrew, and then Andrew and Philip went to tell Jesus.
12.23: Jesus answered them, «The hour has come for the Son of Man to be glorified.
12.24: Truly, truly, I tell you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it remains alone,
12.25: But if it dies, it bears much fruit. Whoever loves their life will lose it, and whoever hates their life in this world will keep it for eternal life.
12.26: If anyone wants to be my servant, he must follow me, and where I am, there my servant will be also. If anyone serves me, my Father will honor him.
12.27: Now my soul is troubled, and what shall I say? Father, deliver me from this hour. But it was for this very reason that I came to this hour.
12.28: Father, glorify your name. «And a voice came from heaven: «I have glorified it, and I will glorify it again.»”
12.29: The crowd that was there and had heard it said, "It's thunder," others said, "An angel spoke to him."«
12.30: Jesus said, «This voice was not spoken for my sake, but for yours.”.
12.31: This is now the judgment of this world; now the Prince of this world will be cast out.
12.32: And I, when I am lifted up from the earth, will draw all people to myself.»
12.33: What he was saying was to indicate the kind of death he was destined to die.
12.34: The crowd answered him, «We have heard from the Law that the Christ remains forever; how then can you say, »The Son of Man must be lifted up’? Who is this Son of Man?”
12.35: Jesus said to them, «The light is among you only for a short time. Walk while you have the light, lest darkness overtake you: the one who walks in the darkness does not know where he is going.
12.36: "While you have the light, believe in the light, so that you may become children of light." Jesus said these things, and then went away and was out of their sight.
12.37: Although he had performed so many miracles in their presence, they did not believe in him:
12.38: So that the word of the prophet Isaiah might be fulfilled, saying, «Lord, who has believed our word? And to whom has the arm of the Lord been revealed?»
12.39: Therefore, they could not believe, because Isaiah also said:
12.40: «"He has blinded their eyes and hardened their hearts, so that they might not see with their eyes, understand with their hearts, turn back, and I would heal them."»
12.41: Isaiah said these things when he saw the glory of the Lord and spoke about him.
12.42: Many, however, even among the members of the Sanhedrin, believed in him, but because of the Pharisees, they did not acknowledge him publicly, for fear of being expelled from the synagogue.
12.43: For they loved the glory of men more than the glory of God.
12.44: Then Jesus raised his voice and said, «Whoever believes in me does not believe in me but in the one who sent me,
12.45: And whoever sees me sees the one who sent me.
12.46: I came into the world as a light, so that whoever believes in me may not remain in darkness.
12.47: If anyone hears my word and does not keep it, I do not judge him/her, for I did not come to judge the world, but to save the world.
12.48: He who despises me and does not receive my word, he has his judge: the very word that I have spoken. It will judge him on the last day.
12.49: For I have not spoken on my own authority, but the Father who sent me has himself commanded me what to say and what to teach.
12.50: And I know that his commandment is eternal life. Therefore, the things I say, I say just as my Father taught me.»

13.1: Before the Passover festival, Jesus, knowing that his hour had come to depart from this world to his Father, having loved his own who were in the world, loved them to the end.
13.2: During dinner, when the devil had already put it into the heart of Judas, son of Simon Iscariot, to betray him,
13.3: Jesus, who knew that his Father had given all things into his hands and that he had come from God and was going to God,
13.4: He got up from the table, put down his garment and, having taken a cloth, he put it around his waist.
13.5: Then he poured water into the basin and began to wash his disciples’ feet and to dry them with the towel that was wrapped around him.
13.6: So he came to Simon Peter, and Peter said to him, "Lord, are you going to wash my feet?"«
13.7: Jesus answered him, «What I am doing you do not understand now, but you will understand later.»
13.8: Peter said to him, «No, you shall never wash my feet.» Jesus answered him, «If I do not wash you, you have no part with me.»
13.9: Simon Peter said to him, «Lord, not only my feet, but also my hands and my head.»
13.10: Jesus said to him, «He who has bathed needs only to wash his feet; his whole body is clean. And you are clean too, though not all of you.»
13.11: For he knew who it was who was going to betray him, and so he said, «Not all of you are clean.»
13.12: After he had washed their feet and put on his garment, he returned to the table and said to them, «Do you understand what I have done to you?
13.13: You call me Master and Lord, and you are right, for that is what I am.
13.14: If I then, your Lord and Teacher, have washed your feet, you also ought to wash one another’s feet.
13.15: For I have given you an example, so that you also may do as I have done to you.
13.16: Truly, truly, I say to you, a servant is not greater than his master, nor is a messenger greater than the one who sent him.
13.17: If you know these things you are happy, provided you practice them.
13.18: I do not say this of all of you; I know those whom I have chosen, but the Scripture must be fulfilled: “He who eats bread with me has lifted up his heel against me.”.
13.19: I am telling you now, before it happens, so that when it does happen you may know who I am.
13.20: »Truly, truly, I tell you, whoever receives me receives the one who sent me.”
13.21: Having said this, Jesus was troubled in spirit and he expressly declared, «Truly, truly, I say to you, one of you will betray me.»
13.22: The disciples looked at one another, not knowing who he was talking about.
13.23: Now one of them was lying on Jesus' chest; it was the one whom Jesus loved.
13.24: Simon Peter then signaled to him, saying, "Who is this man he is talking about?"«
13.25: The disciple, leaning back against Jesus' chest, said to him, "Lord, who is it?"«
13.26: Jesus answered, "It is the one to whom I will give the dipped piece." And having dipped the bread, he gave it to Judas Iscariot, Simon's son.
13.27: As soon as Judas had seized him, Satan entered into him, and Jesus said to him, "What you are going to do, do quickly."«
13.28: None of those at the table understood why he was saying that to him.
13.29: Some thought that, since Judas had the money bag, Jesus wanted to tell him: "Buy what you need for the feast" or "Give something to the poor."«
13.30: Judas, having taken the piece of bread, hurried out. It was night.
13.31: When Judas had gone out, Jesus said, "Now the Son of Man has been glorified, and God has been glorified in him.".
13.32: If God has been glorified in him, God will also glorify him in himself, and he will glorify him soon.
13.33: My grandchildren, I am with you only for a little while longer. You will look for me, and just as I told the Jews they could not come where I am going, I tell you the same now.
13.34: I give you a new commandment: that you love one another, that as I have loved you, you also love one another.
13.35: This is how everyone will know that you are my disciples, if you love one another.»
13.36: Simon Peter said to him, «Lord, where are you going?» Jesus answered, «Where I am going, you cannot follow me now, but you will follow me later.
13.37: »Lord,” Peter said to him, “why can I not follow you now? I will lay down my life for you.”
13.38: Jesus answered him, «You will lay down your life for me. Truly, truly, I tell you, the rooster will not crow until you have denied me three times.»

14.1: «Do not let your hearts be troubled. You believe in God, believe also in me.”.
14.2: In my Father’s house are many rooms; if it were not so, I would have told you, for I am going there to prepare a place for you.
14.3: And when I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again and take you to myself, so that where I am you may be also.
14.4: And you know the way to where I'm going.»
14.5: Thomas said to him, "Lord, we do not know where you are going, so how can we know the way?"«
14.6: Jesus said to him, «I am the way, the truth and the life, no one comes to the Father except through me.
14.7: If you had known me, you would have known my Father also. Now you know him and have seen him.»
14.8: Philip said to him, «Lord, show us the Father, and that will be enough for us.»
14.9: Jesus answered him, «Have I been with you a long time, and you still do not know me? Philip, whoever has seen me has seen the Father also. How can you say, ‘Show us the Father’?”.
14.10: Do you not believe that I am in the Father and the Father is in me? The words that I say to you I do not speak on my own authority; rather, it is the Father who dwells in me who does these works.
14.11: Believe me when I say that I am in the Father and the Father is in me.
14.12: Believe it at least because of these works. Truly, truly, I say to you, whoever believes in me will also do the works that I do, and greater works than these.,
14.13: because I am going to the Father and whatever you ask the Father in my name, I will do, so that the Father may be glorified in the Son.
14.14: If you ask me to do something on my behalf, I will do it.
14.15: If you love me, keep my commandments.
14.16: And I will ask the Father, and he will give you another Advocate to help you and be with you forever.
14.17: This is the Spirit of truth, whom the world cannot receive, because it neither sees him nor knows him; but you know him, for he dwells among you and will be in you.
14.18: I will not leave you orphans, I will come to you.
14.19: A little while longer and the world will no longer see me, but you will see me, because I live and you will live.
14.20: On that day you will know that I am in my Father and you are in me and I am in you.
14.21: »Whoever has my commandments and keeps them is the one who loves me. The one who loves me will be loved by my Father, and I too will love them and show myself to them.”
14.22: Judas, not Iscariot, said to him, "Lord, why is it that you wish to reveal yourself to us and not to the world?"«
14.23: Jesus answered him, «If anyone loves me, he will keep my word, and my Father will love him, and we will come to him and make our home with him.
14.24: Whoever does not love me will not keep my words. And the word that you hear is not mine, but the Father's who sent me.
14.25: I have told you these things while I am still with you.
14.26: But the Advocate, the Holy Spirit, whom my Father will send in my name, will teach you all things and will remind you of everything I have told you.
14.27: I leave you peace; I give you my peace, I do not give it as the world gives. Do not let your hearts be troubled and do not be afraid.
14.28: You heard me say, “I am going away and I am coming back to you.” If you loved me, you would be glad that I am going to the Father, for my Father is greater than I.
14.29: And now I have told you these things before they happen, so that when they do happen you may believe.
14.30: I will hardly speak with you anymore, for the Prince of this world is coming and he has no hold over me.
14.31: But so that the world may know that I love my Father and that I do according to the commandment my Father gave me, rise, let us go from here.»

15.1: «I am the true vine, and the Father is the vinedresser.”.
15.2: Every branch in me that does not bear fruit he cuts off, and every branch that does bear fruit he prunes, so that it will bear more fruit.
15.3: You are already clean because of the word that I have spoken to you.
15.4: Remain in me and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit by itself, unless it remains in the vine, so neither can you, unless you remain in me.
15.5: I am the vine, you are the branches. He who abides in me, and I in him, bears much fruit; for apart from me you can do nothing.
15.6: If anyone does not remain in me, he is thrown away like a branch and withers; such branches are gathered, thrown into the fire and burned.
15.7: If you remain in me and my words remain in you, you will ask whatever you wish, and it will be done for you.
15.8: It is to my Father's glory that you bear much fruit and are my disciples.
15.9: As my Father has loved me, so have I loved you; remain in my love.
15.10: If you keep my commandments, you will abide in my love, just as I have kept my Father’s commandments and abide in his love.
15.11: I have told you these things so that my joy may be in you and that your joy may be complete.
15.12: This is my commandment: that you love one another as I have loved you.
15.13: There is no greater love than to lay down one's life for one's friends.
15.14: You are my friends if you do what I command.
15.15: I no longer call you servants, because a servant does not know his master’s business, but I have called you friends, for everything that I learned from my Father I have made known to you.
15.16: You did not choose me, but I chose you and appointed you so that you might go and bear fruit, and that your fruit might last, and that the Father might give you whatever you ask in my name.
15.17: What I command you is to love one another.
15.18: If the world hates you, know that it hated me first.
15.19: If you were of the world, the world would love what belonged to it. But because you are not of the world, but I have chosen you out of the world, therefore the world hates you.
15.20: Remember the word I spoke to you: “A servant is not greater than his master.” If they persecuted me, they will persecute you also; if they kept my word, they will keep yours also.
15.21: But they will do all these things to you because of my name, because they do not know the one who sent me.
15.22: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would be without sin, but now their sin is without excuse.
15.23: He who hates me hates my Father also.
15.24: If I had not done among them works which no one else has done, they would be without sin; but now they have seen and they hate me and my Father.
15.25: But this happened so that the word written in their Law might be fulfilled: “They hated me without a cause.”.
15.26: »When the Advocate, whom I will send to you from the Father—the Spirit of truth who proceeds from the Father—comes, he will testify about me. And you also must testify, for you have been with me from the beginning.”

16.1: I have told you these things so that you will not be offended.
16.2: They will drive you out of the synagogues, and indeed the hour is coming when whoever kills you will think he is offering God an acceptable sacrifice.
16.3: And they will act in this way because they have not known my Father or me.
16.4: But I have told you this so that, when the time comes, you will remember that I told you this.
16.5: I did not tell you about this from the beginning because I was with you. And now that I am going to the one who sent me, none of you asks me, «Where are you going?»
16.6: But because I told you these things, sadness filled your heart.
16.7: However, I tell you the truth: it is to your good that I go away, for if I do not go away, the Advocate will not come to you; but if I go, I will send him to you.
16.8: And when he comes, he will convict the world concerning sin, righteousness, and judgment:
16.9: concerning sin, because they did not believe in me,
16.10: Regarding justice, because I am going to the Father and you will see me no longer,
16.11: concerning the judgment, because the Prince of this world has [already] been judged.
16.12: I still have many things to tell you, but you cannot carry them now.
16.13: When the Advocate, the Spirit of truth, comes, he will guide you into all the truth. For he will not speak on his own authority, but he will speak whatever he hears, and he will tell you what is yet to come.
16.14: He will glorify me, because he will receive what is mine and will declare it to you.
16.15: All that the Father has is mine. That is why I said that he will receive what is mine and declare it to you.
16.16: »A little while longer and you will no longer see me, and a little while longer and you will see me, because I am going to my Father.”
16.17: Some of his disciples then said to one another, «What does he mean by saying, »In a little while you will no longer see me, and in a little while you will see me again, because I am going to the Father’?”
16.18: So they were saying, "What does 'a little longer' mean? We don't know what it means."«
16.19: Jesus knew that they wanted to question him and said to them, «Are you asking one another what I said: ‘In a little while you will no longer see me, and in a little while you will see me again.
16.20: Truly, truly, I say to you, you will weep and mourn while the world rejoices; you will grieve, but your grief will turn into joy.
16.21: A woman, when she gives birth, is in pain because her time has come. But when she has given birth to the child, she no longer remembers her pains, for she rejoices that a child has been born into the world.
16.22: So you too are now in affliction, but I will see you again and your heart will rejoice and no one will take your joy from you.
16.23: On that day you will no longer ask me anything. Truly, truly, I tell you, whatever you ask the Father in my name, he will give it to you.
16.24: Until now you have asked nothing in my name: ask and you will receive, so that your joy may be complete.
16.25: I have told you these things in parables. The hour is coming when I will no longer speak to you in parables, but will speak to you plainly about the Father.
16.26: On that day you will ask in my name, and I am not telling you that I will pray to the Father on your behalf.
16.27: For the Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and have believed that I came from the Father.
16.28: I came from the Father and entered the world; now I am leaving the world and going back to the Father.»
16.29: His disciples said to him, «Look, you are speaking plainly and without using any imagery.
16.30: Now we see that you know everything and that you don't need anyone to question you; that's why we believe you came from God.»
16.31: Jesus answered them, «Do you now believe?»
16.32: The hour is coming, and is now here, when you will be scattered, each to his own home, and you will leave me alone. Yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.
16.33: I have told you these things, so that in me you may have peace. You have trials in this world, but take heart! I have overcome the world.

17.1: Having said this, Jesus lifted up his eyes to heaven and said, «Father, the hour has come; glorify your Son, so that your Son may glorify you,
17.2: Since you have given him authority over all flesh, so that to all those whom you have given him, he may give eternal life.
17.3: Now this is eternal life: that they know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom you have sent.
17.4: I have glorified you on earth, I have completed the work that you gave me to do.
17.5: And now to you, Father, glorify me in your presence with the glory that I had in your presence before the world existed.
17.6: I have revealed your name to the men whom you gave me out of the world. They were yours, and you gave them to me, and they have kept your word.
17.7: They now know that everything you gave me came from you.,
17.8: For the words that you gave me, I gave them. And they received them and truly recognized that I came from you, and they believed that it was you who sent me.
17.9: It is for them that I pray. I am not praying for the world, but for those whom you have given me, for they are yours.
17.10: For all that is mine is yours and all that is yours is mine, and I am glorified in them.
17.11: I am no longer in the world. They are in the world, but I am coming to you. Holy Father, protect them by the power of your name, which you have given me, so that they may be one, even as we are one.
17.12: While I was with them, I preserved them in your name. I have guarded those you gave me, and not one of them has been lost except the one destined for destruction, so that the Scripture might be fulfilled.
17.13: Now I come to you and make this prayer while I am still in the world, so that they may have the fullness of my joy in themselves.
17.14: I gave them your word, and the world hated them because they are not of the world, just as I am not of the world.
17.15: I am not asking you to remove them from the world, but to keep them from evil.
17.16: They are not of this world, just as I myself am not of this world.
17.17: Sanctify them in the truth: your word is truth.
17.18: As you sent me into the world, so I have sent them into the world.
17.19: And I sanctify myself for them, so that they too may be truly sanctified.
17.20: I pray not only for them, but also for those who, through their preaching, will believe in me.
17.21: So that they may all be one, just as you, Father, are in me and I am in you, so that they also may be in us, so that the world may believe that you have sent me.
17.22: And I have given them the glory that you gave me, so that they may be one, as we are one, I in them and you in me.
17.23: So that they may be perfectly one and that the world may know that you sent me and that you loved them as you loved me.
17.24: Father, I want those whom you have given me to be with me where I am, so that they may see the glory that you have given me, because you loved me before the creation of the world.
17.25: Righteous Father, the world has not known you, but I have known you, and these have known that it is you who sent me.
17.26: And I have made your name known to them, and I will continue to make it known, so that the love with which you have loved me may be in them, and I in them.»

18.1: After saying this, Jesus and his disciples went beyond the Kidron Valley, where there was a garden, into which he and his disciples entered.
18.2: Judas, who betrayed him, also knew this place, because Jesus had often gone there with his disciples.
18.3: Having therefore taken the cohort and guards provided by the Pontiffs and the Pharisees, Judas came there with lanterns, torches and weapons.
18.4: Then Jesus, knowing all that was going to happen to him, came forward and said to them, «Whom are you looking for?»
18.5: They answered him, «Jesus of Nazareth.« He said to them, »I am Jesus of Nazareth.” Now Judas, who betrayed him, was there with them.
18.6: So when Jesus said to them, «It is I,» they drew back and fell to the ground.
18.7: He asked them again, "Who are you looking for?" And they said, "Jesus of Nazareth."«
18.8: Jesus answered, «I told you, I am he. If then you are looking for me, let these men go.»
18.9: He said this to fulfill the word he had spoken: «I have lost none of those you gave me.»
18.10: Then Simon Peter, who had a sword, drew it and struck the servant of the high priest, cutting off his right ear: this servant's name was Malchus.
18.11: But Jesus said to Peter, «Put your sword back into its sheath. Shall I not then drink the cup that my Father has given me?»
18.12: Then the cohort, the tribune, and the Jewish guards seized Jesus and bound him.
18.13: They first took him to Annas because he was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, who was high priest that year.
18.14: Now, Caiaphas was the one who had given this advice to the Jews: "It is better for one man to die for the people."«
18.15: However, Simon Peter followed Jesus, along with another disciple. This disciple, being known to the high priest, went with Jesus into the courtyard of the high priest.,
18.16: But Peter had remained outside the door. So the other disciple, who was known to the high priest, went out, spoke to the doorkeeper, and brought Peter in.
18.17: The servant girl who kept the door said to Peter, "Aren't you also one of this man's disciples?" He said, "I am not."«
18.18: The servants and guards were gathered around a fire, because it was cold and they were keeping warm. Peter was also standing with them, keeping warm.
18.19: The high priest questioned Jesus about his disciples and his teachings.
18.20: Jesus answered him, «I have spoken openly to the world; I have always taught in the synagogue and in the temple, where all the Jews come together, and I have said nothing in secret.
18.21: Why do you question me? Ask those who heard me what I told them; they know what I taught.»
18.22: At these words, one of the guards who was there slapped Jesus, saying, "Is that how you answer the high priest?"«
18.23: Jesus answered him, «If I have spoken wrongly, show me what wrong I said; but if I have spoken rightly, why do you strike me?»
18.24: Anne had sent Jesus bound to Caiaphas, the high priest.
18.25: Now Simon Peter was there, warming himself. They said to him, «Aren’t you also one of his disciples?» He denied it and said, «I am not.»
18.26: One of the high priest's servants, a relative of the man whose ear Peter had cut off, said to him, "Didn't I see you with him in the garden?"«
18.27: Pierre denied it again, and immediately the rooster crowed.
18.28: They led Jesus from Caiaphas's house to the Praetorium: it was morning. But they themselves did not enter the Praetorium, so as not to defile themselves and so that they could eat the Passover.
18.29: Pilate then went out to them and said, "What accusation do you bring against this man?"«
18.30: They replied, "If he weren't a criminal, we wouldn't have handed him over to you."«
18.31: Pilate said to them, «Take him yourselves and judge him according to your law.« The Jews answered him, «We are not permitted to put anyone to death.»
18.32: So that the word which Jesus had spoken might be fulfilled, when he indicated the kind of death by which he was going to die.
18.33: Pilate, therefore, having returned to the Praetorium, summoned Jesus and said to him, «Are you the king of the Jews?»
18.34: Jesus answered, «Do you say this of your own accord, or did others tell you this about me?»
18.35: Pilate replied, "Am I a Jew? Your own nation and the chief priests have handed you over to me. What have you done?"«
18.36: Jesus answered, «My kingdom is not of this world. If my kingdom were of this world, my servants would have fought to prevent my arrest by the Jewish leaders. But my kingdom is not from the world.»
18.37: Pilate said to him, «So you are a king?» Jesus answered, «You say that I am a king. For this purpose I was born and for this purpose I came into the world to testify to the truth: everyone who belongs to the truth listens to my voice.»
18.38: Pilate said to him, «What is truth?» Having said this, he went out again to the Jews and said to them:
18.39: «For my part, I find no guilt in him. But it is the custom that at the Passover festival I release someone to you. Do you want me to release the King of the Jews?»
18.40: Then they all shouted again, "Not him, but Barabbas!" Now Barabbas was a robber.

19.1: Then Pilate took Jesus and had him flogged.
19.2: And the soldiers twisted together a crown of thorns, placed it on his head, and clothed him in a purple robe.,
19.3: Then, approaching him, they said, "Hail, King of the Jews," and they slapped him.
19.4: Pilate went out again and said to the Jews, "Here I am bringing him out to you, so that you may know that I find no guilt in him."«
19.5: Jesus then came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the scarlet robe, and Pilate said to them, «Here is the man.»
19.6: When the chief priests and the guards saw him, they cried out, "Crucify him! Crucify him!" Pilate said to them, "Take him yourselves and crucify him, for I find no guilt in him."«
19.7: The Jews answered him, «We have a law, and according to our law he must die, because he claimed to be the Son of God.»
19.8: Having heard these words, Pilate was even more frightened.
19.9: And going back into the praetorium, he said to Jesus, "Where are you from?" But Jesus gave him no answer.
19.10: Pilate said to him, "Do you refuse to speak to me? Do you not know that I have the power to set you free and the power to crucify you?"«
19.11: Jesus answered, «You would have no power over me if it were not given to you from above. Therefore, the one who handed me over to you is guilty of a greater sin.»
19.12: From that moment, Pilate sought to free him. But the Jews shouted, saying, "If you free him, you are no friend of Caesar; whoever claims to be king declares himself against Caesar."«
19.13: Pilate, having heard these words, had Jesus led out and sat down on his judgment seat at the place called Lithostrotos and in Hebrew Gabbatha.
19.14: It was the day of Preparation for Passover, and it was about the sixth hour. Pilate said to the Jews, "Here is your king."«
19.15: But they began to shout, "Die! Die! Crucify him!" Pilate said to them, "Shall I crucify your king?" The chief priests answered, "We have no king but Caesar."«
19.16: So he handed him over to be crucified.
19.17: And they took Jesus and led him away. Jesus, carrying his cross, came outside the city to the place called Calvary, in Hebrew Golgotha,
19.18: That is where they crucified him and two others with him, one on each side and Jesus in the middle.
19.19: Pilate also had an inscription made and placed at the top of the cross. It read: «Jesus of Nazareth, the King of the Jews.»
19.20: Many Jews read this sign, for the place where Jesus had been crucified was near the city and the inscription was in Hebrew, Greek, and Latin.
19.21: But the chief priests of the Jews said to Pilate, "Do not write: 'The King of the Jews,' but that he himself said: 'I am the King of the Jews.'"«
19.22: Pilate replied, "What I wrote, I wrote."«
19.23: After crucifying Jesus, the soldiers took his clothes and divided them into four parts, one for each of them. They also took his tunic, which was seamless, made of a single piece of fabric from top to bottom.
19.24: So they said to one another, «Let us not tear it, but cast lots for it to see whose it shall be,» so that the Scripture might be fulfilled: «They divided my garments among them and cast lots for my robe.» And so the soldiers did.
19.25: Near the cross of Jesus stood his mother and his mother's sister, Mary, wife of Cleopas, and Mary Magdalene.
19.26: When Jesus saw his mother and the disciple whom he loved standing nearby, he said to his mother, «Woman, behold your son.»
19.27: Then he said to the disciple, "Here is your mother." And from that hour the disciple took her into his home.
19.28: After this, Jesus, knowing that everything was now finished in order to fulfill the Scripture, said, «I thirst.»
19.29: There was a vase full of vinegar there; the soldiers filled a sponge with it and, having fixed it to the end of a hyssop stalk, they brought it close to his mouth.
19.30: When Jesus had received the vinegar, he said, "It is finished," and bowing his head, he gave up his spirit.
19.31: Now, since it was the Preparation Day, lest the bodies should remain on the cross during the Sabbath, for that Sabbath day was very solemn, the Jews asked Pilate that the legs of the crucified men be broken and that they be taken down.
19.32: So the soldiers came and broke the legs of the first man, and then of the other who had been crucified with him.
19.33: But when they came to Jesus and saw that he was already dead, they did not break his legs.
19.34: But one of the soldiers pierced his side with his spear and immediately blood and water flowed out.
19.35: And he who saw it testifies to it, and his testimony is true, and he knows that he speaks the truth, so that you also may believe.
19.36: For these things happened so that the Scripture might be fulfilled: «Not one of his bones will be broken.»
19.37: And it is written elsewhere: "They will look on the one they have pierced."«
19.38: After this, Joseph of Arimathea, who was a disciple of Jesus, but secretly for fear of the Jews, asked Pilate for permission to take away the body of Jesus. And Pilate gave his consent. So he came and took away the body of Jesus.
19.39: Nicodemus, who had first come to Jesus at night, also came, bringing a mixture of myrrh and aloes, about one hundred pounds.
19.40: So they took the body of Jesus and wrapped it in linen cloths with the spices, according to the Jewish burial custom.
19.41: Now in the place where Jesus was crucified there was a garden, and in the garden a new tomb in which no one had yet been laid.
19.42: It was there, because of the Jewish Preparation Day, that they laid Jesus, because the tomb was nearby.

20.1: On the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene went to the tomb early in the morning, before darkness had dissipated, and she saw the stone removed from the tomb.
20.2: So she ran and went to Simon Peter and the other disciple whom Jesus loved, and said to them, «They have taken the Lord out of the tomb, and we do not know where they have laid him.»
20.3: Peter went out with the other disciple and they went to the tomb.
20.4: They were both running together, but the other disciple ran faster than Peter and reached the tomb first.
