20.5: And, having bent down, he saw the shrouds laid on the ground, but he did not go in.
20.6: Simon Peter, who was following him, arrived in turn and entered the tomb.
20.7: He saw the linen cloths lying on the ground and the cloth that covered Jesus’ head, not lying with the linen cloths, but rolled up in another place.
20.8: Then the other disciple, who had reached the tomb first, also went in, and he saw and he believed.
20.9: because they did not yet understand the Scripture, according to which he must rise from the dead.
20.11: However, Mary stood outside the tomb, shedding tears, and weeping, she bent down towards the tomb.,
20.12: and she saw two angels dressed in white, sitting where the body of Jesus had been, one at the head and the other at the feet.
20.13: And they said to her, "Woman, why are you weeping?" She said to them, "Because they have taken away my Lord, and I do not know where they have put him."«
20.14: Having said these words, she turned around and saw Jesus standing there, and she did not know that it was Jesus.
20.15: Jesus said to her, «Woman, why are you weeping? Whom are you looking for?» She, thinking he was the gardener, said to him, «Sir, if you have carried him away, tell me where you have laid him and I will go and get him.»
20.16: Jesus said to her, "Mary." She turned around and said to him in Hebrew, "Rabboni," which means "Teacher."«
20.17: Jesus said to her, «Do not cling to me, for I have not yet ascended to the Father. But go to my brothers and tell them, »I am ascending to my Father and your Father, to my God and your God.’”
20.18: Mary Magdalene went and told the disciples that she had seen the Lord and that he had said these things to her.
20.19: On the evening of that same day, the first of the week, when the disciples were together, with the doors locked for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood among them and said, «Peace be with you.»
20.20: Having said this, he showed them his hands and his side. The disciples were filled with joy when they saw the Lord.
20.21: He said to them a second time, «Peace be with you. As the Father has sent me, so I send you.»
20.22: After saying this, he breathed on them and said, «Receive the Holy Spirit.»
20.23: «"Those whose sins you forgive are forgiven, and those whose sins you retain are retained."»
20.24: But Thomas, one of the twelve, the one called Didymus, was not with them when Jesus came.
20.25: The other disciples told him, «We have seen the Lord.» But he said to them, «Unless I see the nail marks in his hands and put my finger where the nails were, and my hand into his side, I will not believe.»
20.26: Eight days later, while the disciples were still in the same place, and Thomas with them, Jesus came, the doors being locked, and standing in their midst, he said to them, «Peace be with you.»
20.27: Then he said to Thomas, «Put your finger here and see my hands; reach out your hand and put it into my side. Stop doubting and believe.»
20.28: Thomas replied, "My Lord and my God."«
20.29: Jesus said to him, «Because you have seen me, [Thomas], you have believed; blessed are those who have not seen and yet have believed.»
20.30: Jesus performed many other miracles in the presence of his disciples, which are not recorded in this book.
20.31: But these were written so that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that by believing you may have life in his name.

21.1: After this, Jesus appeared again to his disciples by the Sea of Tiberias, and he appeared in this way:
21.2: Simon Peter, Thomas called Didymus, Nathanael, who was from Cana in Galilee, the sons of Zebedee and two others of his disciples, were together.
21.3: Simon Peter said to them, «I am going fishing.» They said to him, «We will go with you.» So they went out and got into the boat, but they caught nothing that night.
21.4: When morning came, Jesus was found on the shore, but the disciples did not know that it was Jesus.
21.5: And Jesus said to them, «Children, do you have nothing to eat?» “No,” they replied.
21.6: He told them, "Cast the net on the right side of the boat and you will find some." They cast it, but they could not pull it in because of the large number of fish.
21.7: Then the disciple whom Jesus loved said to Peter, «It is the Lord.» When Simon Peter heard that it was the Lord, he put on his outer garment and girdle (for he was naked) and threw himself into the sea.
21.8: The other disciples came with the boat, for they were not far from land, only about two hundred cubits, dragging the net full of fish.
21.9: When they went ashore, they saw burning coals there, fish placed on top, and bread.
21.10: Jesus said to them, «Bring some of the fish you have just caught.»
21.11: Simon Peter got into the boat and hauled the net ashore, which was full of one hundred and fifty-three large fish, and although there were so many, the net did not break.
21.12: Jesus said to them, «Come and eat.» And none of the disciples dared to ask him, «Who are you?» because they knew it was the Lord.
21.13: Jesus approached and taking the bread, he gave it to them, and did the same with the fish.
21.14: This was already the third time that Jesus had appeared to his disciples since he had risen from the dead.
21.15: When they had finished eating, Jesus said to Simon Peter, «Simon, son of John, do you love me more than these?» He answered him, «Yes, Lord, you know that I love you.» Jesus said to him, «Feed my lambs.»
21.16: He said to him a second time, «Simon, son of John, do you love me?» Peter answered him, «Yes, Lord, you know that I love you.» Jesus said to him, «Feed my lambs.»
21.17: He said to him for the third time, «Simon, son of John, do you love me?» Peter was grieved because Jesus asked him the third time, «Do you love me?» and he answered him, «Lord, you know everything; you know that I love you.» Jesus said to him, «Feed my sheep.»
21.18: «Truly, truly, I say to you, when you were younger you dressed yourself and went where you wanted; but when you are old you will stretch out your hands and someone else will dress you and lead you where you do not want to go.»
21.19: He said this, indicating by what death Peter was to glorify God. And after saying this, he added, «Follow me.»
21.20: Peter turned around and saw the disciple whom Jesus loved coming up behind him, the one who, during the Last Supper, had leaned back on his chest and said to him, «Lord, who is this who is going to betray you?»
21.21: So Peter, having seen him, said to Jesus, «Lord, what will become of this man?»
21.22: Jesus said to him, «If I want him to stay until I come, what is that to you? You follow me.»
21.23: So the rumor spread among the brothers that this disciple would not die. Yet Jesus had not told him that he would not die, but: «If I want him to remain until I come, what is that to you?»
21.24: This is the same disciple who testifies to these things and who wrote them down, and we know that his testimony is true.
21.25: Jesus did many other things as well; if they were recorded in detail, I do not think the whole world could contain the books that would have to be written.
